---
title: "starting_out"
author: "anuj s"
date: "2026-02-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r warning = F, message = F}

## packages
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(AmphiNom)
library(rgbif)
library(devtools)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthhires)
library(readxl)

## Retrieve the paths specific to the machine I am using.
host <- Sys.info()[["nodename"]]

if (host == "ANUJ-PC") {
  base_path <- "C:/Users/Anuj Shinde/OneDrive - Australian National University"
} else if (host == "RSB-088702") {
  base_path <- "C:/Users/u8007407/OneDrive - Australian National University"
} else if (host == "rsb0001147") {
  base_path <- "onedrive://bf21b313-5537-4a7c-a787-0949e4040392/OneDrive/Project%20DataFiles/biodiv_data_gaps"
} else {
  stop("Unknown machine — please define paths for this system.")
}

## Set all paths (external or internal)
wd <- list()
wd$raw <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                     "biodiv_data_gaps","raw_files") #general folder
wd$data <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                     "biodiv_data_gaps","data") #general folder
wd$output <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                       "biodiv_data_gaps","output") #general folder
wd$starting_out <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                       "biodiv_data_gaps","starting_out") #script specific folder

sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE) # disables s2 geometry in the sf package.

```

## Load the main ZSI faunal list

```{r }

## Load ZSI faunal checklist
zsi_fauna <- read_csv(file.path(wd$raw, "zsi_faunal_list/zsi_faunal_list.csv"))

## Remove duplicates
zsi_fauna <-  zsi_fauna %>%
  distinct(species, .keep_all =T) %>%
  rename(scientificName = species) %>% 
  dplyr::select(scientificName, kingdom)

## Match ZSI names to the GBIF backbone (took ~7 hours to complete)
zsi_backbone <- map2_dfr(
  zsi_fauna$scientificName,
  zsi_fauna$kingdom,
  ~ rgbif::name_backbone(name = .x, kingdom = .y),
  .id = NULL
)

zsi_backbone <- read_csv(file.path(wd$data, "zsi_matched_to_gbif/zsi_backbone.csv"))


```

## Filtering the focal taxonomic groups from the matched list

```{r }

## Load the matched species list
matched_list <- read_excel(file.path(wd$data, "zsi_matched_to_gbif/zsi_matched_to_backbone.xlsx"), 
                        sheet = "zsi_backbone", 
                        col_types = "text") %>%
  filter(phylum %in% c("Arthropoda", "Mollusca", "Chordata", "NA")) 

### To filter out unmatched species (Na in species column) and to retain only Animalia.
## I will remove the duplicated species

matched_list <- matched_list %>%
  filter(class == "Amphibia") %>% drop_na(species) %>%
  distinct(speciesKey, .keep_all = T)

counts <- sapply(as.integer(matched_list$speciesKey[1:10]), function(key) {
  occ_count(speciesKey = key, country ="IN")
})
sum(counts)
# Your vector of species keys from ZSI matching
species_keys <- c(2440934, 2440935, 2440936, ...)  # Your matched keys

# Create download request
download_key <- occ_download(
  pred_in("taxonKey", species_keys),
  pred("country", "IN"),  # Optional: restrict to India
  pred("hasCoordinate", TRUE),  # Optional: only georeferenced records
  format = "SIMPLE_CSV",
  user = "your_gbif_username",
  pwd = "your_password",
  email = "your_email@example.com"
)

```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
